meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first project.
content.answer_block: Version control is the lifeblood of software engineering. Without Git, your project is a ticking time bomb of lost progress. Using a SaaS starter kit integrates versioning at the architecture level, ensuring your code is tracked, modular, and deployment-ready from the very first commit. It is the ultimate student shortcut.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours crafting the perfect logic, only to realize you’ve overwritten your best work with a single, panicked 'undo.' This is the 'no version control' trap—a silent project killer that stalls thousands of student portfolios before they even launch. It isn't just about saving files; it's about building a professional history.

But what if you could bypass the pain of manual file management? By leveraging a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you inherit a battle-tested architecture that enforces versioning, authentication, and database migrations out of the box. Stop building in the dark and start engineering with the tools that the pros use to scale.

steps.title: Initialize Repository with Starter Kit
steps.description: Establish a clean base using a SaaS starter kit to ensure Git is active from the first line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modern SaaS project using a Next.js starter kit that includes pre-configured Git hooks and TypeScript settings.

steps.title: Automate Git Workflow
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate your commit messages, ensuring every feature is documented clearly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Husky and lint-staged to prevent non-compliant code from being committed to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup git hooks and commit message linting'

steps.title: Database Schema Versioning
steps.description: Migrate your database state alongside your code, ensuring your environment always matches your logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database migration script for a User and Project entity using Prisma to keep schema changes in sync.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma migration for new user schema'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Setup
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI provider to automate testing every time you push code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit tests and build checks on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create github actions ci pipeline'

steps.title: Deploy to Production Environment
steps.description: Push your version-controlled project to a live URL, completing the professional software lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment environment variables and output a deployment summary for Vercel integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'deploy project to production and verify environment variables'

pro_tips: Commit early and often; a 'work in progress' commit is infinitely better than a lost hour of debugging.
Use descriptive branch names like 'feat/auth-module' rather than 'fix1' to track the evolution of your features.
Always run your linter before committing to keep your commit history clean of formatting noise.
Treat your README.md as a living document that explains not just what the app does, but how to set it up.
Leverage Claude Code to write your commit messages for you—it understands your diffs better than you do.
resources: Git Best Practices for Beginners: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your first version is deployed, add a new feature branch, implement a test suite, and practice merging your changes back into the main branch to master the git flow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the foundation to build, track, and scale your ideas like a senior engineer. Don't let your code sit idle on your local machine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
